scenarios_detailed.reason: OS AIX 7.2 is OUTDATED. IBM AIX 7.2 is in its declining support phase. While IBM offers technology refresh updates, AIX 7.2 is aging and not recommended for new deployments. OS update is required.
OS is a proprietary non-standard Unix system (AIX 7.2). Migration to standard Linux (RHEL/Ubuntu) would improve cloud compatibility and reduce licensing costs.
Application runs on proprietary Unix OS (AIX/HP-UX/Solaris) which is excluded from ARM migration.
No application server is used by this application.
Application is on-premise with a legacy/monolithic architecture (1-tier). Lift & Shift is possible but refactoring may be needed first for optimal cloud deployment.
Application uses a proprietary legacy Unix OS (AIX/Solaris) which is excluded from containerization.
Application has a 1-tier architecture with limited modularity. Refactoring and decoupling would improve maintainability and cloud-readiness.

Database IBM Db2 unknown is OUTDATED. IBM Db2 version not specified. IBM Db2 LTS versions have long support but the version is indeterminate; assessed as OUTDATED based on legacy context. Upgrade is recommended.
Application uses IBM Db2, a proprietary licensed database. Migration to PostgreSQL would reduce license costs.
Outdated/EOL application components detected: FORTRAN 2018 (OUTDATED). These should be updated to current supported versions.
